children.heading: Establishment of Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Matching Grant Program
children.label: a Establishment of Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Matching Grant Program
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a Community Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Matching Grant Program under which the Secretary may, subject to the availability of appropriated funds, make grants to provide matching funds for the development, expansion, or continuation of qualified return preparation programs assisting applicable taxpayers and members of underserved populations.

children.label: i
children.text: wages or salaries of persons coordinating the activities of the program,

children.label: ii
children.text: developing training materials, conducting training, and performing quality reviews of the returns prepared under the program,

children.label: iii
children.text: equipment purchases, and

children.label: iv
children.text: vehicle-related expenses associated with remote or rural tax preparation services,

children.label: B
children.text: outreach and educational activities described in subsection (c)(2)(B), and

children.label: C
children.text: services related to financial education and capability, asset development, and the establishment of savings accounts in connection with tax return preparation.

children.label: A
children.text: the salary (including fringe benefits) of individuals performing services for the program,

children.label: B
children.text: the cost of equipment used in the program, and

children.label: C
children.text: other ordinary and necessary costs associated with the program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each applicant for a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may reasonably require.

children.label: A
children.text: assistance to applicable taxpayers, with emphasis on outreach to, and services for, such taxpayers,

children.label: B
children.text: taxpayer outreach and educational activities relating to eligibility and availability of income supports available through this title, including the earned income tax credit, and

children.label: C
children.text: specific outreach and focus on one or more underserved populations.

children.heading: Amounts taken into account
children.label: 3 Amounts taken into account
children.text: In determining matching grants under this section, the Secretary shall only take into account amounts provided by the qualified return preparation program for expenses described in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: to ensure the program is carrying out the purposes of this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: to determine whether the program meets such program adherence standards as the Secretary shall by regulation or other guidance prescribe.

children.heading: Additional requirements for grant recipients not meeting program adherence standards
children.label: 2 Additional requirements for grant recipients not meeting program adherence standards
A
children.text: is awarded a grant under this section, and

children.label: i
children.text: not to meet the program adherence standards described in paragraph (1)(B), or

children.label: ii
children.text: not to be otherwise carrying out the purposes of this section,

children.label: A
children.text: which provides assistance to individuals, not less than 90 percent of whom are applicable taxpayers, in preparing and filing Federal income tax returns,

children.label: B
children.text: which is administered by a qualified entity,

children.label: C
children.text: in which all volunteers who assist in the preparation of Federal income tax returns meet the training requirements prescribed by the Secretary, and

children.label: D
children.text: which uses a quality review process which reviews 100 percent of all returns.

children.label: i
children.text: is an eligible organization,

children.label: ii
children.text: is in compliance with Federal tax filing and payment requirements,

children.label: iii
children.text: is not debarred or suspended from Federal contracts, grants, or cooperative agreements, and

children.label: iv
children.text: agrees to provide documentation to substantiate any matching funds provided pursuant to the grant program under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: an institution of higher education which is described in section 102 (other than subsection (a)(1)(C) thereof) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section, and which has not been disqualified from participating in a program under title IV of such Act,20 U.S.C. 1002

children.label: ii
children.text: an organization described in section 501(c) and exempt from tax under section 501(a),

children.label: I
children.text: a county or municipal government agency, and

children.label: II
children.text: an Indian tribe, as defined in section 4(13) of the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 (), including any tribally designated housing entity (as defined in section 4(22) of such Act ()), tribal subsidiary, subdivision, or other wholly owned tribal entity,25 U.S.C. 4103(13)25 U.S.C. 4103(22)

children.label: iv
children.text: a local, State, regional, or national coalition (with one lead organization which meets the eligibility requirements of clause (i), (ii), or (iii) acting as the applicant organization), or

children.label: I
children.text: a State government agency, or

children.label: II
children.text: an office providing Cooperative Extension services (as established at the land-grant colleges and universities under the Smith-Lever Act of ).May 8, 1914

children.heading: Applicable taxpayers
children.label: 3 Applicable taxpayers
children.text: The term “applicable taxpayer” means a taxpayer whose income for the taxable year does not exceed an amount equal to the completed phaseout amount under section 32(b) for a married couple filing a joint return with three or more qualifying children, as determined in a revenue procedure or other published guidance.

children.heading: Underserved population
children.label: 4 Underserved population
children.text: The term “underserved population” includes populations of persons with disabilities, persons with limited English proficiency, Native Americans, individuals living in rural areas, members of the Armed Forces and their spouses, and the elderly.

children.heading: Duration of grants
children.label: 1 Duration of grants
children.text: Upon application of a qualified return preparation program, the Secretary is authorized to award a multi-year grant not to exceed 3 years.

children.heading: Aggregate limitation
children.label: 2 Aggregate limitation
children.text: Unless otherwise provided by specific appropriation, the Secretary shall not allocate more than $30 million per fiscal year (exclusive of costs of administering the program) to grants under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall promote tax preparation through qualified return preparation programs through the use of mass communications and other means.

children.heading: Provision of information regarding qualified return preparation programs
children.label: 2 Provision of information regarding qualified return preparation programs
children.text: The Secretary may provide taxpayers information regarding qualified return preparation programs receiving grants under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: advise taxpayers of the availability of, and eligibility requirements for receiving, advice and assistance from qualified low-income taxpayer clinics receiving funding under section 7526, and

children.label: B
children.text: provide information regarding the location of, and contact information for, such clinics.
